content:
I’ve taken all of this into account and structured it into a logical, well-ordered guide that covers everything we discussed while making it engaging, actionable, and directly applicable to Derek’s work. I also made sure to expand on the section about studying inspirations, emphasizing the importance of varied influences so that he doesn’t just mimic one source but instead builds a unique artistic voice.

Everything below flows as a step-by-step creative process, alternating between theory and activity to reinforce each lesson.

The Writing Guide for Derek: Exploration, Structure, and Momentum

Introduction: The Underground Work (The Iceberg Mentality)

Before we dive into structure, embrace the idea that most writing isn’t meant to be seen by anyone but you. Think of Tarantino’s approach—he writes way more than what makes it onto the final screenplay. He knows every character’s history, every backstory, every detail—even if it never explicitly appears in the film.

This kind of underground writing is your foundation. It helps you make better decisions because you understand the world and characters beyond the surface.
